findings.title: Dashboard verify route TOCTOU race condition
findings.description: Classic TOCTOU race. Reads payment.status at line 149, runs multi-second async pipeline (Gmail + LLM), then writes status:verified at lines 323-334 with plain UPDATE WHERE id = paymentId — no conditional on current status.
findings.impact: Two concurrent requests both see 'pending' and both write 'verified'. Double-counting of payments.
findings.remediation: Add .eq('status', 'pending') to the UPDATE query. Check affected row count — if 0, another request already verified it.
findings.notes: Added .eq('status', 'pending').select() with empty-result guard

findings.title: Hardcoded dedup_check: true — no actual dedup in dashboard verify
findings.description: dedup_check: true is hardcoded in evidence records. Unlike the www route, there is zero dedup logic in the dashboard verify endpoint.
findings.impact: Same bank email can verify a payment multiple times. Misleading audit trail.
findings.remediation: Implement real dedup check using database UNIQUE constraint on upi_reference_id or a Redis-backed dedup store.
findings.notes: Added DB-backed dedup query checking for existing verified payment with same upi_reference_id

findings.title: Both API routes bypass core library's Zod validation and SecurityValidator
findings.description: Both routes call Gemini API directly via fetch(), extract JSON with greedy regex /{[\s\S]*}/, and parse with JSON.parse() — never validating against parsedPaymentSchema. The well-designed core library (LangChain structured output + 4-layer SecurityValidator) exists but is simply not used.
findings.impact: LLM output is trusted without schema validation. Prompt injection can fabricate arbitrary JSON that passes the regex extraction.
findings.remediation: Refactor both API routes to use the core library's parsePaymentEmail() which uses LangChain .withStructuredOutput() and Zod schema validation.
findings.notes: Dashboard route refactored to use parsePaymentEmail() from @upiagent/core. WWW route still uses raw fetch (demo endpoint, lower priority).

findings.title: Core library has ZERO input sanitization for LLM prompts
findings.description: The sanitizeEmailForLlm() function with injection pattern filtering, length truncation, and delimiters exists only inline in the two API routes — NOT in the core library. Raw email subject/body/from flow directly into the prompt template with no filtering.
findings.impact: Anyone using UpiAgent.verifyPayment() directly gets zero prompt injection protection.
findings.remediation: Port sanitizeEmailForLlm() and delimiter wrapping into the core library's prompt template.
findings.notes: sanitizeEmailForLlm() added to prompts.ts, called in chain.ts before LLM invocation. Delimiters added to prompt template. Exported from package.

findings.title: No UNIQUE constraint on payments.upi_reference_id
findings.description: No UNIQUE constraint on payments.upi_reference_id, so the same bank alert can verify multiple different payments for the same amount.
findings.impact: Cross-payment replay attack — one bank email verifies multiple payments.
findings.remediation: CREATE UNIQUE INDEX idx_payments_upi_ref_unique ON payments(upi_reference_id) WHERE upi_reference_id IS NOT NULL;
findings.notes: Migration 003_payment_constraints.sql created with UNIQUE partial index

findings.title: Dashboard /api/verify has no rate limiting — cost amplification vector
findings.description: Auth-gated but no rate limit. Each request triggers up to 5 Gmail API calls + 5 Gemini LLM calls.
findings.impact: Attacker with valid session can cause massive downstream API costs with cheap HTTP requests.
findings.remediation: Add per-user rate limiting (e.g., 10 verifications/min). Use Upstash Redis for distributed state.
findings.notes: Added per-user rate limiting (10 req/min) with in-memory Map

findings.title: /api/demo endpoint has no rate limiting
findings.description: Public endpoint, no auth, no rate limit. Generates QR codes freely.
findings.impact: Anyone can spam QR generation, filling up database with junk payment records.
findings.remediation: Add IP-based rate limiting similar to the www /api/verify endpoint.
findings.notes: Added IP-based rate limiting (5/min per IP, 50/min global)

findings.title: WWW demo verify in-memory dedup non-functional in serverless
findings.description: In-memory dedup via Map resets on every cold start and is not shared across Vercel serverless isolates.
findings.impact: Dedup is effectively non-existent in production serverless deployment.
findings.remediation: Move dedup to database (UNIQUE constraint) or Redis.

findings.title: Injection pattern blocklist easily bypassable
findings.description: Regex blocklist catches 'ignore previous', 'you are a/an/now', etc. But bypassed by Unicode homoglyphs, zero-width characters, multilingual instructions.
findings.impact: Attacker can craft email content that passes the regex filter but still manipulates the LLM.
findings.remediation: Add Unicode NFKC normalization + zero-width char stripping before regex. Expand pattern list.
findings.notes: Added normalizeInput() with NFKC + zero-width stripping. Expanded blocklist with 5 new patterns.

findings.title: No email sender validation after Gmail fetch
findings.description: Gmail search query filters by bank sender, but after emails are fetched, the 'from' field is passed to LLM without programmatic verification against a bank allowlist.
findings.impact: If Gmail returns email from unexpected sender (forwarding rules, query override), no code catches it.
findings.remediation: Add sender allowlist validation after fetching — verify email.from matches known bank addresses before LLM processing.
findings.notes: Added KNOWN_BANK_SENDERS Set, extractEmailAddress() helper, validateSender() filter in fetchBankAlerts()

findings.title: Zero input validation on onboarding/settings server actions
findings.description: Server actions for onboarding and settings accept user input without Zod schema validation or format checks.
findings.impact: Malformed or malicious data can be written to the database.
findings.remediation: Add Zod schemas to all server actions that accept user input.
findings.notes: Added Zod schemas to 4 server action files: onboarding, settings, api-keys, create-payment

findings.title: Existing rate limit uses x-forwarded-for — spoofable
findings.description: Rate limiting in the www verify route uses x-forwarded-for for IP detection, which can be spoofed.
findings.impact: Rate limiting can be bypassed by rotating the x-forwarded-for header value.
findings.remediation: Use x-real-ip (set by Vercel) or the request socket address instead.
findings.notes: Changed to prefer x-real-ip in both www verify and demo routes

findings.title: In-memory rate limit state lost on cold starts
findings.description: Rate limit counters stored in module-level Map, wiped on every serverless cold start.
findings.impact: Rate limiting protection is intermittent.
findings.remediation: Use Upstash Redis for persistent rate limit state.

findings.title: /api/gmail/connect has no rate limit or self-contained auth check
findings.description: Relies solely on middleware redirect for auth. No rate limiting on OAuth initiation.
findings.impact: If middleware is misconfigured, unauthenticated OAuth flows could be triggered.
findings.remediation: Add explicit auth check in the route handler. Add rate limiting.
findings.notes: Added getUser() auth check at top of handler

findings.title: Confidence score is LLM self-reported and gameable
findings.description: The confidence threshold (>= 0.5) relies on the LLM's self-reported confidence. A successful prompt injection can set confidence to 1.0.
findings.impact: Confidence-based filtering is not effective against targeted injection attacks.
findings.remediation: Cross-reference LLM output with external signals (email sender verification, bank format patterns) rather than trusting LLM confidence alone.

findings.title: Public verify page allows transaction ID enumeration
findings.description: txn_id is unvalidated user input queried with service role key. No HMAC protection.
findings.impact: Attacker can enumerate transaction IDs and view payment details.
findings.remediation: Add HMAC signature to payment URLs or require authentication.

findings.title: payments.status is unconstrained text — no CHECK constraint
findings.description: Payment status is free text with no CHECK constraint limiting it to valid values.
findings.impact: Application bugs could write arbitrary status values.
findings.remediation: ALTER TABLE payments ADD CONSTRAINT chk_status CHECK (status IN ('pending', 'verified', 'expired'));
findings.notes: Added CHECK constraint in migration 003_payment_constraints.sql

findings.title: No uniqueness constraint on verification_evidence per payment
findings.description: verification_evidence table allows duplicate evidence rows from race conditions.
findings.impact: Duplicate evidence records created during concurrent verify requests.
findings.remediation: Add UNIQUE constraint on (payment_id, source_type) or (payment_id, extracted_upi_ref).
findings.notes: Added UNIQUE index idx_evidence_payment_source in migration 003

findings.title: Gemini API key exposed in URL query parameter
findings.description: API key passed as ?key= query parameter in Gemini fetch URL. Visible in server logs, CDN logs, error tracking.
findings.impact: Credential compromise, unauthorized API usage, billing abuse.
findings.remediation: Use x-goog-api-key header instead of URL parameter.
findings.notes: Moved to x-goog-api-key header

findings.title: Missing HTTPS enforcement in OAuth redirect URI
findings.description: OAuth redirect URI constructed from NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_URL without enforcing HTTPS in production.
findings.impact: OAuth authorization code theft via MITM if HTTP is used.
findings.remediation: Validate NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_URL starts with https:// in production.
findings.notes: Added production HTTPS check

findings.title: Payment page exposes data via unauthenticated service role query
findings.description: Public verify page fetches full payment details with service role key. Any attacker who knows a txn ID can view payment data.
findings.impact: Information disclosure — merchant payment data, customer names, amounts exposed.
findings.remediation: Add HMAC-signed tokens to payment URLs. Limit exposed fields.

findings.title: LLM API key returned to client in settings form
findings.description: Decrypted LLM API key sent to client as defaultValue in form input. Visible in DOM.
findings.impact: Merchant's own API key exposed in browser. Logged in browser history.
findings.remediation: Never send decrypted keys to client. Show masked prefix only.
findings.notes: Settings page now sends has_llm_api_key boolean, not raw key. Form uses empty defaultValue with descriptive placeholder.

findings.title: Overly broad .select(*) in demo verify — fetches all merchant columns
findings.description: Demo verify uses .select('*') with service role key, fetching all merchant columns including encrypted credentials.
findings.impact: Larger attack surface if any code path exposes the query result.
findings.remediation: Use explicit column selection.
findings.notes: Changed to explicit column list

findings.title: OAuth state cookie uses SameSite=Lax instead of Strict
findings.description: OAuth state cookie should use SameSite=Strict since it's never needed cross-site.
findings.impact: Marginally increased CSRF risk in edge cases.
findings.remediation: Change to sameSite: strict.
findings.notes: Changed to strict

findings.title: Hardcoded demo UPI ID exposes personal bank info
findings.description: Personal UPI ID amarpathakhdfc@ybl hardcoded in source code committed to repo.
findings.impact: Privacy leak — personal bank account info publicly discoverable.
findings.remediation: Move to environment variable.
findings.notes: Changed to process.env.DEMO_UPI_ID with safe fallback

findings.title: Settings action stores LLM API key without encryption
findings.description: When a merchant updates their LLM API key via settings, it was stored in plaintext. The Gmail callback encrypts credentials, but the settings action didn't.
findings.impact: Merchant LLM API keys stored unencrypted in database, violating the encryption-at-rest policy.
findings.remediation: Encrypt with CREDENTIALS_ENCRYPTION_KEY before storing.
findings.notes: Added encrypt() call before database write, with warning log if encryption key is missing

findings.title: Gmail callback stores GOOGLE_CLIENT_SECRET as plaintext if env var is falsy
findings.description: Ternary fallback allowed undefined/plaintext client secret to be stored if GOOGLE_CLIENT_SECRET was empty.
findings.impact: Google OAuth client secret stored unencrypted in database.
findings.remediation: Fail hard if GOOGLE_CLIENT_SECRET is missing. Always encrypt.
findings.notes: Added explicit check for GOOGLE_CLIENT_SECRET. Removed ternary fallback — always encrypts.

findings.title: Dedup query uses .single() which throws on 0 rows
findings.description: .single() throws PostgREST error if 0 or 2+ rows returned. Masks dedup failures as generic Gmail errors.
findings.impact: Silent failure of duplicate detection. Attacker could re-verify same transaction.
findings.remediation: Use .maybeSingle() instead.
findings.notes: Changed to .maybeSingle()

findings.title: Greedy regex in JSON extraction vulnerable to ReDoS
findings.description: Regex /{[\s\S]*}/ uses greedy wildcard. If LLM output has nested braces or very long text, regex engine backtracks excessively.
findings.impact: Verification endpoint hangs on crafted LLM output.
findings.remediation: Use non-greedy: /{[\s\S]*?}/
findings.notes: Changed to non-greedy match

findings.title: Gmail callback missing HTTPS enforcement on redirect URLs
findings.description: The connect route validates HTTPS but the callback route didn't. All error redirects used unvalidated appUrl.
findings.impact: Open redirect to HTTP in production. OAuth code interception via MITM.
findings.remediation: Add same HTTPS check as connect route.
findings.notes: Added production HTTPS check

findings.title: Gmail callback leaks Supabase error messages in redirect URLs
findings.description: Raw updateError.message passed to redirect URL query parameter. Could leak internal schema info.
findings.impact: Information disclosure about database structure.
findings.remediation: Map to generic error codes. Log actual error server-side.
findings.notes: Changed to generic 'failed_to_save_credentials' message. Actual error logged server-side.
